title: sideroxylon peninsulare
intro: Sideroxylon peninsulare, also known as the Queensland boxwood or eastern boxwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and triterpenoid compounds. The health benefits of Sideroxylon peninsulare include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral and antifungal activities. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional remedies for various health conditions.
